meta.name: Helix — Job Search Coach
meta.status: Coach model: Discord-first review, manual apply, Helix Coach NL channel, schedule-aware digests
meta.description: Helix scrapes job listings from multiple sources, scores them with keyword and LLM matching, deduplicates cross-source, and can tailor resumes/cover letters via LLM with ATS compliance. Primary workflow is Discord: /review queue, approve → apply card (URL, match, I Applied / Remind Me (2h) / Resume Tips / Skip). User applies in the browser; no CLI/browser auto-submit of ATS forms. Optional #helix-coach channel: natural-language job URL analysis (fit, viability, tips), cover-letter drafts, LinkedIn outreach, resume Q&A (Ollama or OpenAI). SQLite tracks jobs; Excel tracker.xlsx has Applications (full queue), Applied (submitted apps, refreshed on apply and on /tracker), and Stats. Optional Gmail read-only monitoring. Digests, scans, and reminders follow config/profile.yaml schedule (timezone + apply_windows).

user_profile.note: Fictional example only. Real job targets, visa, and PII belong in config/profile.yaml (gitignored); copy from config/profile.template.yaml.
user_profile.visa_status: Example — set your real status in profile.yaml
user_profile.sponsorship_priority: Configure sponsorship preferences in profile.yaml; Helix can tag jobs with sponsorship likelihood for informed decisions.

daily_target.quality_policy: Quality is king. Tailored documents should pass the quality gate (LLM-scored >= 7/10) where document generation is used. Coach workflow emphasizes thoughtful manual applications over volume.

phases.phase: Profile config, API scrapers (Adzuna, Jooble, The Muse), SQLite DB, keyword scoring, sponsorship detection, Excel tracker with formatting

LLM semantic scoring (OpenAI gpt-4o-mini), cross-source dedup (thefuzz), Greenhouse API scraper (29 boards), Lever API scraper (25 companies), Google Jobs via SerpAPI, LinkedIn Playwright scraper (50/session cap), enhanced score blending (35% keyword + 65% LLM)

Daily planner (top 25 with diversity rules + sponsor priority), LLM company research briefs, ATS-compliant resume tailoring (.docx + .pdf), cover letter generation, quality gate (score >= 7/10, retry up to 2x)

Discord bot: review queue, ApplyCardView (I Applied, Remind Me (2h), Resume Tips, Skip), accountability (/today, /follow_up, /goal, /note, /schedule, /strategy, /myday), charts, /tracker Excel export, reminders + schedule_events, schedule_tick in user TZ. Helix Coach channel (#helix-coach auto-detect or helix_coach_channel_id): coach_channel.py for pasted job URLs + NL career help. No ATS form auto-submit; user applies in browser.

Gmail OAuth2 auth (read-only scope), inbox polling with keyword + LLM classification, fuzzy email-to-job matching, automatic status updates in DB, APScheduler for periodic scans/email checks/daily planning

dependency.name: playwright
dependency.purpose: Optional: LinkedIn job discovery scraping only (not used for form auto-apply)

dependency.name: discord.py
dependency.purpose: Discord bot: primary UI for review, apply cards, digests

dependency.name: openai
dependency.purpose: LLM API for scoring, docs, company research, email parsing

dependency.name: requests
dependency.purpose: API calls (Adzuna, Jooble, Greenhouse, Lever, SerpAPI)

dependency.name: cloudscraper
dependency.purpose: HTTP fetch for job page checks and coach URL snapshots

dependency.name: beautifulsoup4
dependency.purpose: HTML parsing for job descriptions and coach channel

dependency.name: rich
dependency.purpose: Terminal UI, progress bars, tables, panels

dependency.name: docx2pdf
dependency.purpose: Convert .docx to .pdf on Windows (requires MS Word)

dependency.name: thefuzz
dependency.purpose: Fuzzy matching for dedup and email-to-job matching

architecture.data_flow: Job Board APIs + Web Scrapers (LinkedIn may use Playwright for discovery) -> Dedup and Normalize -> SQLite DB (jobs, status_history, reminders, schedule_events) -> Job Scorer (keyword + LLM + sponsorship) -> Optional: resume/cover tailoring + quality gate for prepared applications -> Human Review in Discord (/review, approve/reject) -> Apply card: user opens URL, applies manually, taps I Applied or Remind Me -> Excel export (Applications + Applied + Stats); Gmail read-only monitor updates statuses -> Optional Helix Coach (#helix-coach): URL fetch + fitness + LLM assistance

rule.title: ATS (Applicant Tracking System) Compliance - ALWAYS ENFORCED
rule.description: All resumes and cover letters MUST follow ATS-friendly formatting rules. ATS systems parse documents programmatically; non-compliant formatting causes applications to be silently rejected before a human ever sees them.

guidelines.guideline: Use .docx format as primary (most ATS-compatible). Generate .pdf as secondary.
Use standard, simple fonts: Calibri, Arial, Garamond, or Cambria. Size 10-12pt for body, 14-16pt for name.
NO tables, columns, text boxes, images, graphics, icons, or headers/footers. ATS cannot parse content inside these elements.
Use single-column layout only. Multi-column layouts break ATS parsing.
Use standard section headings exactly as: "Professional Summary", "Experience", "Education", "Skills", "Certifications", "Projects". Do not get creative with heading names.
Use simple bullet points (•). Avoid special characters, symbols, or custom bullet styles.
Do not use "Objective" sections. Use "Professional Summary" instead (2-3 lines max).
Mirror exact keywords and phrases from the job description. If the JD says "CI/CD pipelines", use "CI/CD pipelines" not "continuous integration and deployment".
Spell out abbreviations on first use, then use abbreviation: "Applicant Tracking System (ATS)". Exception: universally known acronyms (SQL, API, HTML, CSS, AWS).
Use reverse chronological order for experience and education.
Include exact job title matches when truthful. If JD says "SDET" and you were an SDET, use "SDET".
Quantify achievements: "Reduced test execution time by 40%", "Automated 200+ test cases". Numbers parse well and stand out to both ATS and humans.
Include both spelled-out and abbreviated versions of technologies where space permits: "Amazon Web Services (AWS)", "Structured Query Language (SQL)".
Do NOT stuff keywords unnaturally. The resume must read well to a human recruiter too. Balance keyword optimization with readability.
Tailor the Professional Summary for each application to match the specific role.
Cover letters should also be ATS-friendly: plain text formatting, standard structure (greeting, 3 paragraphs, closing), include key role-specific keywords naturally.
Always address to specific hiring manager if available. Otherwise use "Dear Hiring Team". Never use "To Whom It May Concern".
Name files as: FirstName_LastName_Resume_CompanyName.docx and FirstName_LastName_CoverLetter_CompanyName.docx. Some ATS index by filename.

rule.title: Quality Over Quantity
rule.description: Every application must be individually researched and tailored. The system generates 25 applications/day but each one should look like it was hand-crafted. The quality gate enforces this by scoring documents and rejecting generic output.

rule.title: Never Fabricate Experience
rule.description: The LLM may only rephrase, reorder, and emphasize existing experience from the base resume. It must NEVER invent new skills, technologies, job titles, companies, or achievements. Keyword optimization is about phrasing, not fiction.

rule.title: Manual application submission
rule.description: The user submits applications in the employer's ATS or site in their own browser. Helix provides the link, match context, and tracking via Discord; it does not drive Playwright to submit forms. Resume tips and document prep remain available where implemented.

rule.title: Visa Sponsorship Prioritization
rule.description: Jobs from known H1B sponsors get scoring bonus. Jobs explicitly stating "no sponsorship" are still included but ranked lower. Never exclude a job solely based on sponsorship status unless the JD explicitly says "must be US citizen" or "no visa sponsorship available".

rule.title: Respectful Scraping
rule.description: Use randomized delays (3-10s) between browser actions. Prefer API-based sources. Limit LinkedIn/Indeed scraping to 50 jobs per session. Cache everything in SQLite to avoid re-scraping. Respect robots.txt where applicable.

rule.title: Secrets and Privacy
rule.description: API keys and tokens in config/secrets.yaml, config/credentials.json, config/gmail_token.json. Personal data in config/profile.yaml. All are .gitignored. Never log API keys.

rule.title: Email Monitoring Safety
rule.description: Gmail monitor is READ-ONLY. It never sends, deletes, modifies, or moves emails. It only reads subject lines and bodies to classify application status. Every classification is logged for manual review.

project.directory_structure: job-automation/ PROJECT_CONTEXT.xml # Authoritative product context (coach model) README.md # Overview; points to SETUP.md for full install SETUP.md # Canonical step-by-step setup (clone → bot → secrets → profile) GUIDE.md # CLI power-user reference (not primary onboarding) docs/BETA_LAUNCH.md # Pre-ship checklist requirements.txt pytest.ini / tests/ # Apply-flow and integration smoke tests scripts/ test_helix_integration.py # Schedule + matching + voice (no Discord) verify_bot_startup.py # Schedule preview (same idea as bot console) config/ profile.yaml / profile.template.yaml # Skills, schedule (profile.yaml gitignored) search_config.yaml # Scraper queries and toggles secrets.yaml (gitignored) src/ cli.py # CLI: scan, stats, prepare, monitor, etc. (no apply subcommand) scheduler.py # APScheduler for CLI-side periodic tasks discord/ bot.py # Slash commands, schedule_tick, digests, reminders views.py # ReviewView, ApplyCardView, batch review coach_channel.py # NL coach (#helix-coach): URLs, cover letter, LinkedIn, Q&A voice.py # Helix tone strings charts.py utils/schedule.py # Timezone, apply windows, digest triggers apply/ job_checker.py # HTTP check for expired jobs / competition (cloudscraper) url_resolver.py # Resolve aggregator URLs to ATS URLs scrapers/ # Adzuna, Jooble, The Muse, Greenhouse, Lever, Google Jobs, LinkedIn matching/ # scorer, llm_scorer, profile_fitness, sponsorship, dedup, ... quality/ # daily_planner, company_research, quality_gate documents/ # resume_tailor, cover_letter email/ # Gmail read-only monitor tracker/ # db.py (reminders, schedule_events), excel_tracker (Applications/Applied/Stats) output/ # jobs.db (gitignored pattern), generated artifacts

notes_for_future_sessions.note: Coach model is live: primary UX is Discord (python -m src.discord.bot). Restart bot after code changes. Slash commands include /schedule, /strategy, /myday, /today, /follow_up, /goal, /note, /tracker. Create #helix-coach for Helix Coach NL channel (auto-detected). No CLI/browser ATS auto-submit.

Setup for new users: follow SETUP.md end-to-end. Fill config/profile.yaml (timezone, apply_windows), config/secrets.yaml (Discord token; Ollama default LLM). Optional: search_config.yaml, credentials.json. Flow: /scan -> /review -> approve -> apply card -> manual apply -> I Applied.
The user is on F1-OPT visa. This is time-sensitive - they need a full-time sponsored position. Sponsorship priority is baked into scoring and planning. Never compromise on this.

ATS compliance is enforced in resume_tailor.py and cover_letter.py via LLM system prompts. The quality_gate.py scores documents on ATS compliance, keyword match, relevance, and readability. Documents must score >= 7/10 or they are regenerated (up to 2 retries).
API keys are ALL configured in config/secrets.yaml: OpenAI (LLM scoring + docs), Adzuna (free), Jooble (free), SerpAPI (free 100/mo). Greenhouse, Lever, The Muse, and LinkedIn don't need keys.
There is no automated ATS form submission in-repo. Tracking and reminders happen in Discord and SQLite; the user submits applications on employer sites manually.

Gmail monitoring (Phase 5) requires config/credentials.json from Google Cloud Console. This is optional - the system works without it. When configured, it only uses gmail.readonly scope and NEVER sends, deletes, or modifies emails.

The scheduler (src/scheduler.py) can run the full pipeline on autopilot: scan every N hours, check email every N minutes, generate daily plan at a set time. Use 'python -m src.cli schedule' to start it.
GUIDE.md documents CLI commands (scan, prepare, monitor, schedule). Onboarding is SETUP.md only.
